title: Hittite Laws on bees and hives
description: The Hittite Laws tariff theft of bees in a swarm and theft of bee hives, making apiculture property an early written legal category.

title: Mishnah on beehives and nuisance
description: Mishnah Bava Batra treats beehives, bees, swarms, honeycombs, and mustard-near-bees conflict as sale and neighbor-law subjects.

title: Salic Law on stolen bees
description: The Salic Law gives stolen bees their own theft title, showing that early successor-kingdom tariff law made bee property a named legal object.

title: Justinian on bee property
description: Justinian's Institutes and Digest class bees as naturally wild: a swarm is owned by hiving it, and a departed swarm remains owned only while visible and easy to pursue.

title: Rothari on hives and bee trees
description: Rothari's Lombard code distinguishes theft from an apiary vessel from taking bees out of another person's marked tree.

title: The Sickle Murder
description: Song Ci records the first forensic entomology: flies settle on the one blade bearing invisible blood, forcing a confession.

title: Fleta on bee occupation
description: Fleta restates the medieval English rule that bees are wild by nature and become property only through effective enclosure or visible, practicable pursuit.

title: The slugs of Autun
description: An ecclesiastical court proceeds against crop-destroying slugs — one of the earliest documented insect/pest prosecutions.

title: Chassenée's treatise
description: Bartholomew Chassenée writes the first legal treatise on insect prosecution, arguing vermin are 'lay persons' entitled to counsel.

title: Weevils of St-Julien (first trial)
description: A vine-weevil prosecution is dismissed on the theory that the insects are a divine scourge.

title: Weevils of St-Julien (sequel)
description: In an eight-month trial the community offers the weevils a land preserve — an eerie foreshadowing of modern critical-habitat designation.

title: Virginia silk-input mandate
description: Virginia's first assembly requires mulberry planting and silk-flax work, treating colonial silk inputs as statutory economic policy.

title: Virginia mulberry-tree act
description: Colonial Virginia requires landowners to plant mulberry trees for silk production, treating silkworm infrastructure as public economic policy.

title: Blackstone on hived bees
description: Blackstone carries Roman bee doctrine into common-law commentary, treating hived bees as qualified property under both natural and civil law.

title: The Bergeret case
description: Bergeret d'Arbois publishes the first Western post-mortem-interval estimate by insect succession (a mummified infant).

title: Mégnin's La Faune des Cadavres
description: Jean-Pierre Mégnin systematizes the succession model of arthropod waves on remains.

title: Evans documents the animal trials
description: E.P. Evans publishes The Criminal Prosecution and Capital Punishment of Animals, the canonical English-language record of vermin trials.

title: Honeybee Act
description: Congress restricts honey-bee importation, the federal backbone of US apiculture law.

title: The Buck Ruxton 'Jigsaw Murders'
description: The first UK murder conviction using entomology; larvae aged at 12–14 days.

title: Unit 731 Ningbo attack
description: Japan air-drops plague-infected fleas on Ningbo; the single attack causes roughly 1,500 deaths.

title: US Operation Big Itch
description: A Cold War US program tests uninfected flea dispersal for entomological warfare.

title: Biological Weapons Convention
description: The BWC treats insect vectors as covered means of delivery under its General Purpose Criterion.

title: Endangered Species Act
description: The ESA extends federal species protection to arthropods and other invertebrates for the first time.

title: First insect ESA listings
description: The Schaus swallowtail is among the first insects listed under the Endangered Species Act, alongside the Bahama swallowtail.

title: Diamond v. Chakrabarty
description: The Supreme Court holds that human-made living organisms are patentable, opening the door to engineered insects.

title: Home Builders v. Babbitt
description: The D.C. Circuit upholds federal power to protect a purely intrastate fly under the Commerce Clause.

title: Rusty patched bumble bee listed
description: The first continental-US bee gains ESA protection.

title: Kirstin Lobato exoneration
description: The first exoneration based on the *absence* of blowfly colonization, proving death after dark.

title: Oxitec mosquito release
description: EPA permits the experimental release of genetically engineered Aedes aegypti in the Florida Keys.

title: 'Bees are fish' and the Sentience Act
description: Almond Alliance holds bumblebees are 'fish' under CESA the same year the UK Sentience Act covers decapods and cephalopods.

title: Murder hornet eradicated
description: The northern giant hornet is declared eradicated from the US — the first Vespa eradication in North America.

title: Monarch proposal and screwworm detection
description: FWS still treats the monarch listing as proposed while a New World screwworm detection in Texas reactivates sterile-insect eradication.
